learning_outcomes.description: Apply matrix algebra proficiently to derive properties of (and compute) various econometric estimators.
Explain the theory behind the General Linear Regression Model.
Derive and explain the numerical and statistical properties of the OLS, GLS and IV estimators.
Understand the finite sample and asymptotic properties of the OLS, GLS and IV estimators.
Use statistical packages such as R-Studio to compute the various estimators on real world datasets.
Apply the learned theory and methods to the real world.

assessments.task_description: The Final Exam will cover the entire course from vector and matrix algebra through to maximum likelihood estimation. The structure will be similar to the past two years' exams with a series of questions requiring worked answers.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Linear Algebra and Regression

Review of statistical concepts and matrix algebra. We begin to present the regression model using vectors and matrices. This set us up for both moment estimation and least squares estimation.

Learning outcomes: L01
learning_activities.topic_description: Linear Algebra and Regression Review of statistical concepts and matrix algebra. We begin to present the regression model using vectors and matrices. This set us up for both moment estimation and least squares estimation.

learning_activities.topic:
Moment Estimators: Instrumental Variables and GMM

IV Estimation: In this lecture we show how the OLS estimator can be presented as a moment estimator. We then consider different moment conditions that lead to the IV estimator.

Learning outcomes: L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Moment Estimators:  Instrumental Variables and GMM IV Estimation: In this lecture we show how the OLS estimator can be presented as a moment estimator. We then consider different moment conditions that lead to the IV estimator.
